theory.intro: When every result of an experiment has the same chance of happening, the results are called **equally likely outcomes**. Listing them carefully is the first step in working out probabilities.
facts: An **outcome** is one possible result of an experiment.
Outcomes are **equally likely** when no result is favoured over another (a fair coin, a fair die, equal spinner sectors).
A fair coin has $2$ outcomes; a standard die has $6$; an equal spinner has as many outcomes as it has sectors.
steps: Decide what one trial of the experiment is.
List every possible result once, with none left out or repeated.
Check the results are all equally likely (equal sectors, fair object).

foundation.q: List all the equally likely outcomes when you spin this fair spinner.
List all the equally likely outcomes when you toss a fair coin.
List all the equally likely outcomes when you roll a standard six-sided die.
List all the equally likely outcomes when you spin this fair spinner.
A bag holds $1$ red, $1$ blue and $1$ green marble, all the same size. List the equally likely outcomes when one marble is drawn.
List all the equally likely outcomes when you spin this fair spinner.
foundation.a: A, B, C, D, E, F

foundation.q: List all the equally likely outcomes when you draw one card from a set numbered $1$ to $4$ (cards identical except for the number).
List all the equally likely outcomes when you spin this fair spinner.
How many equally likely outcomes are there when you roll a standard die?
List all the equally likely outcomes when you spin this fair spinner.

development.q: List all the equally likely outcomes when you spin this fair spinner numbered with the multiples of $5$.
A fair coin is tossed and then a second fair coin is tossed. List all the equally likely outcomes.
List all the equally likely outcomes when you spin this fair $8$-sector spinner.
A die is rolled. List the equally likely outcomes, then list those that are **even numbers**.
A bag has counters numbered $1$ to $5$, all identical except the number. List all outcomes, then those that are **odd**.
List all the equally likely outcomes when you spin this fair spinner, then state how many there are.
development.a: A, B, C, D, E; $5$ outcomes

development.q: A spinner is split into the four suits hearts, diamonds, clubs and spades in equal parts. List all the equally likely outcomes.
A coin is tossed and a die rolled. How many equally likely outcomes are there? List those starting with heads.
development.a: $12$ outcomes; H1, H2, H3, H4, H5, H6

solution.note: Each coin face with each die face

development.q: List all the equally likely outcomes when you spin this fair spinner, then state which colour names appear.
List all the equally likely outcomes for the sum when two faces of a spinner numbered $1, 2, 3$ are each spun once (give the spins, not the sums).

mastery.q: A spinner has the letters of the word **MATHS**, each on an equal sector. List all the equally likely outcomes and state how many.
mastery.a: M, A, T, H, S; $5$ outcomes

mastery.q: Two fair coins are tossed. List the outcomes, then list those with **exactly one head**.
mastery.a: HH, HT, TH, TT; one head: HT, TH

mastery.q: A spinner is numbered $1$ to $6$ in equal sectors. List the outcomes that are **factors of $6$**.
Explain why the colours on this spinner are **not** equally likely, then say what would make them equally likely.
mastery.a: the sectors are unequal; make all sectors the same size

mastery.q: A die is rolled twice. How many equally likely outcomes are there in total?
From a bag of counters numbered $1$ to $10$ (identical otherwise), list the outcomes that are **multiples of $3$**.
A spinner has $4$ equal sectors. If three of them say **prize**, list the equally likely sector outcomes, not the labels.
A coin is tossed three times. List the $8$ equally likely outcomes.
mastery.a: HHH, HHT, HTH, HTT, THH, THT, TTH, TTT

mastery.q: A spinner numbered $1$ to $4$ is spun twice. List the outcomes where **both spins are the same**.
Two identical dice are rolled. List the ways the **sum is $5$** (as the two faces).
